sections.subtitle: Recent Form Analysis
sections.text: Athletic Club has been struggling with a winless streak of 7 matches, conceding first in all of them and failing to keep a clean sheet in the last 8 games. Mallorca, on the other hand, has shown mixed results with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their last 5 matches, but they managed a narrow 1-0 victory in their most recent game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters between Athletic Club and Mallorca, the matches ended in draws (1-1 and 0-0), indicating tight contests with low-scoring outcomes. Historically, 6 out of the last 7 matches between these teams have had under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Insights
sections.text: Athletic Club (10th, 10 points) has a balanced attack and defense, ranking well in possession (6th) and penalty area entries (2nd), but struggles to convert big chances (16th). Mallorca (20th, 5 points) has scored fewer goals (6 vs. Athletic's 7) and conceded more (11 vs. 8), with weaker overall stats across most metrics.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Athletic Club's home advantage at San Mamés and superior possession stats could give them an edge, though their inability to keep clean sheets is a concern. Mallorca's defensive resilience in recent matches and Vedat Muriqi's form (4 goals in 5 games) might help them secure a result.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at San Mamés saw Athletic Club take a narrow lead against Mallorca with a penalty converted early in the match. Athletic Club dominated much of the possession and created more threatening opportunities, capitalizing on their advantage in the final third. Mallorca struggled to find their rhythm, with limited entries into dangerous areas and no significant chances to show for their efforts. The home side's early goal set the tone, and despite Mallorca's attempts to respond, they couldn't break through a well-organized defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Athletic Club's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club showcased their superiority in the first half, controlling the ball and pushing forward with intent. Their ability to enter the penalty area frequently and win duels, both on the ground and in the air, allowed them to maintain pressure on Mallorca's defense. The early goal from a penalty boosted their confidence, and they consistently looked to exploit spaces in the final third. Key contributions from their forwards and midfielders ensured they held the upper hand throughout most of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's struggles
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca found themselves on the back foot for large parts of the first half, unable to match Athletic Club's intensity. Their defensive structure held at times, but they lacked the cutting edge in attack, failing to register any meaningful attempts on goal. The away side's limited possession in dangerous areas and poor duel-winning stats highlighted their difficulties in gaining a foothold in the game. They will need to regroup and find a way to disrupt Athletic Club's rhythm if they hope to turn things around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was heavily skewed in favor of Athletic Club, particularly in the opening stages where they launched relentless attacking spells. Their persistent pressure in the early minutes led to the penalty and subsequent goal, keeping Mallorca under constant threat. While there were brief moments where Mallorca managed to shift the momentum and build some attacks late in the half, they couldn’t sustain it. Athletic Club regained control toward the end of the half, underlining their dominance in creating dangerous situations.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Athletic Club, Iñaki Williams stands out after scoring the opener and posing a constant threat in attack with his movement and finishing ability. His involvement in key moments makes him a focal point for the second half. On Mallorca’s side, players like Vedat Muriqi will need to step up if they are to find an equalizer, as their attacking output has been minimal so far. Defensive solidity from players like Antonio Raíllo will also be crucial to withstand Athletic Club's pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Athletic Club are likely to continue their aggressive approach, seeking to extend their lead with their strong presence in the final third. Mallorca, trailing by a goal, will need to take more risks, potentially opening up spaces for counter-attacks. Tactical adjustments from the away side could see them push for an equalizer, but they must improve their attacking output significantly. The home crowd at San Mamés will play a role in driving Athletic Club to maintain their intensity.
